content.problemStatement:
You are required to create a program to store and display employee information using a structure in C.

Define a structure named Employee which contains the following details:

Field NameData TypeDescription**name**string (max length 50)Name of the employee**employee_id**integerUnique Employee ID**salary**floatMonthly salaryYour task is to read employee details as input, store them in the structure, and print them in the required output format.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of employees. For each employee, three lines follow: the employee's name (a string of up to 50 characters, may contain spaces), the employee_id (an integer), and the salary (a floating‑point number).
content.outputFormat: For each employee, output a line in the format: Name: <name> ID: <employee_id> Salary: <salary> where <salary> is printed with exactly two digits after the decimal point.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The single employee's details are read and printed exactly as required.
The program stores the name, id and salary and formats the output with two decimal places.
All three fields are displayed in the prescribed order and format.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
Name: XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX ID: 1000000000 Salary: 100000.00
Name: XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX ID: 1000000000 Salary: 100000.00
Name: XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX ID: 1000000000 Salary: 100000.00

hidden.label: uniform/edge case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Name: Same Name ID: 1 Salary: 0.00
Name: Same Name ID: 1 Salary: 0.00

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
Name: Alice ID: 10 Salary: 1234.50
Name: Bob ID: 20 Salary: 0.00
Name: Charlie Brown ID: 30 Salary: 99999.99
Name: Dana ID: 40 Salary: 100000.00

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. For each test case, read the name line using BufferedReader.readLine() to preserve spaces, then read the employee_id and salary lines and parse them to long and double. Use String.format with Locale.US and "%.2f" to format the salary to exactly two decimal places. Accumulate all outputs in a StringBuilder and print at the end. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) time overall, where T is the number of employees.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (output stored in a StringBuilder).
